title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward has impressed in spring training with 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 games.
Max Ferguson leads the Red Sox in RBIs with seven this spring, showing promise despite likely staying in the minors.
Returning catcher Mickey Gasper has contributed six hits, five RBIs, and a home run in 17 at bats.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second on the Bruins in 5-on-5 goals, making him a valuable asset to the team's offense.
Despite being a pending free agent, Arvidsson remains an integral part of the Bruins, avoiding a trade before the deadline.
The Bruins may consider a short-term contract extension for Arvidsson to maintain team stability and scoring depth next season.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots to seek a new left guard after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Isaac Seumalo, 32, from the Steelers, is a top free-agent guard option for the Patriots.
Oregon's Emmanuel Pregnon, a strong draftee option, is highly rated among college guards.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry from Stockton, California is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Make-A-Wish children.
Perry, 48, aims to raise funds and awareness for Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island Make-A-Wish Foundation.
He’s motivated by the resilience of Wish children, having met many over his 15 years of involvement.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots projected to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe with a three-year, $48 million contract, $25 million guaranteed.
Patriots unlikely to sign Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce, who is expected to join the Chargers with a three-year, $80 million deal.
Patriots have $61.6 million cap space available, positioning them to make significant moves in the off-season.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at House of Blues Boston on March 6, 2026, as part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour.'
The Arkansas native's setlist included politically charged songs like 'Sometimes You Bomb Iran' and covers of Radiohead and Black Sabbath.
Welles' 22-song performance featured a mix of original songs and covers, with sharp political lyrics and no intermissions.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida went 2-for-4 with a home run and three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea.
Yoshida's home run came in the third inning, contributing to Japan's advance to a 5-3 lead.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game, wearing a Dodgers uniform, during his visit to Japan.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026, representing Team Eye and Ear.
Raised in Roslindale, Aboujaoude-Quirk has been a patient at Mass Eye and Ear for nearly a decade.
Mass Eye and Ear specializes in eye, ear, nose, and throat research and has a 24-hour emergency department.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team, joining Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson in 2025.
Legler, ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst since 2000, is esteemed for his insights and work ethic.
Legler will cover Celtics-Cavaliers on ABC, expressing enthusiasm for working with Breen and observing Celtics' performance.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg scored 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Mavericks in his TD Garden debut.
Jayson Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in his return from a torn Achilles against the Mavericks.
Flagg received significant applause from fans at TD Garden, a notable moment for the Maine native and Celtics fan.
